title: Deciding on a medical procedure
story: My doctor recommended a preventive surgery that has a 90% success rate but requires months of recovery. I've been going back and forth for weeks. The condition runs in my family - my mother went through it and said the recovery was the hardest thing she's ever done. I'm young and healthy now, so part of me thinks I should just wait and see if I even develop symptoms. But another part of me is terrified that waiting will make it worse and I'll regret not acting early. Every article I read seems to confirm whichever position I'm currently leaning toward, and I feel paralyzed by the decision.
